summary: “Hyena and Raven” by Tony Lelliott and Wiehan de Jager is an English-language children’s story about the friendship between Hyena and Raven and Hyena’s wish to fly with his friend. The simple sentences, large-print style, and very short pages suggest it targets early primary readers (roughly grades 1–2) or beginning English learners. Based on the animal characters and folktale-like setup, it appears rooted in an African storytelling tradition, using a straightforward, repetitive narrative to support early literacy and themes of friendship and aspirations.
